settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Manage tracked keywords and the monitoring scheduler. Defaults: every 6 hours, single keyword.

help.title: How this works
help.subtitle: Signal architecture, why some verdicts end up Unclear, how monitoring runs.

intro.title: What the dashboard tracks
intro.body: Every scheduler tick fetches the top-10 Google SERP for a branded query (default: 'starcasino', geo Netherlands), scrapes each result via headless Chromium, then classifies the domain into one of four buckets. Snapshots are stored in SQLite — the history chart shows how the mix shifts over time.

categories.title: Categories
categories.official: The brand domain itself ranking (starcasino.nl) — instantly decisive, +100 from the rule signal.
categories.affiliate: A third-party site that monetises in the brand's favour: the primary CTA resolves to starcasino.nl with an affiliate tag (btag, aff_id…). Most review portals belong here.
categories.thief: Most threatening: the page ranks on the brand query but the CTA sends users to OTHER casinos (jacks.nl, tonybet.nl…). The brand is just SEO bait; monetisation flows to competitors.
categories.unclear: Not enough signal — informational article, Wikipedia, or a site behind bot protection that returned an empty HTML.

signals.title: Signal architecture
signals.intro: The classifier fuses deterministic rule signals with an LLM verdict (Claude Opus 4.7 via OpenRouter). Signals split into two layers by whether they need a successful page scrape:
signals.serpBody: Visible right in the SERP — can't be hidden by Cloudflare.

serpItems.1: Domain contains the brand stem (starcasino-nl.com) → affiliate/thief prior
serpItems.2: Title fits a review/listicle pattern (review, top 10, beste, alternatieven) → affiliate prior

signals.pageBody: Stronger but brittle: bot protection or JS-injected CTAs zero them all out.

pageItems.0: Outbound links → starcasino.nl or a competitor + affiliate params
pageItems.1: Primary CTA target after redirect resolution
pageItems.2: Anchor says 'brand', href resolves to a competitor → cloaking

signals.fusion: Final category = argmax(rule × 0.6 + LLM × 0.4); if top score < 30 → Unclear. When the LLM says 'Unclear' it's treated as abstention, not a vote against — rule scores then act at weight 1.0.

unclear.title: Why so many Unclear?
unclear.body1: The decisive signal for telling 'affiliate-to-brand' from 'competitor brand thief' is the final domain after clicking the primary CTA and resolving the redirect chain. `final == starcasino.nl + btag=…` → affiliate; `final == jacks.nl + aff_id=…` → thief.
unclear.body2: On the real NL SERP, 6 of 10 top results sit behind Cloudflare/DataDome bot protection. Headless Playwright gets a challenge page with no body content; affiliate links are typically injected via JS after the load event.
unclear.calloutBody: A passive scraper has a floor on Unclear. Production-grade classification needs an active probe — click the CTA in an isolated browser context, await page.waitForURL(). Out of the prototype's scope — documented in wiki/synthesis/phase-plan.

monitoring.title: How monitoring works
monitoring.body: The scheduler (node-cron) lives in a separate worker container and reads the desired cron from SQLite (scheduler_control). Default cadence: every 4 hours (180 runs/month, within the free SerpAPI quota of 250/month). Settings let you change the interval, fire a one-off run, or stop the scheduler. The web container only writes desired_cron — the worker reconciles.

stack.title: Stack
stack.body: Worker (Node 20 + TypeScript): node-cron, SerpAPI / Playwright fallback, headless Chromium, cheerio, better-sqlite3, OpenRouter (Claude Opus 4.7), whoiser for WHOIS enrichment. Web (Fastify + React + Vite + Tailwind + shadcn/ui + Recharts). Infra: Docker Compose with a multi-stage Dockerfile, Caddy reverse proxy + DNS-01 Let's Encrypt, Cloudflare DNS-only.

keyword.title: Tracked keywords
keyword.hint: Each keyword is monitored on every scheduler tick. Switch which one the dashboard shows from the header.
